free_text: Investigation of 28Si levels with the (a,g) and (p,g)
reactions.
3MV accelerator.
Protons beams and alpha (4He+) beams.
For the precise yield curve measurements the
gamma-rays are detected with a 10 cm*10 cm NaI crystal.
The detector is placed at D=3 cm from the target, at an
angle = 55 degr. with respect to the beam axis
Gamma-rays are  detected with Ge(Li) detectors
of 60, 80 and 120 cm2, which have resolutions of 2.5,
1.8 and 3.3 keV at Eg=1.33 MeV, respectively. The
80 cm3 detector is used in an anti-Compton
spectrometer.
SF3 in sub006 was updated.
REACTION codes and COMMON sections in
Sub 002,003 were changed. COMMENT record was added
Data are given as the Number of Counts multiplied by
The Al-27 targets are produced by evaporating aluminium
onto 0.3 mm thick Ta sheets. Before evaporation the Ta
backing is electrically heated under vacuum for several
minutes. The Al targets  contain 2.5+-0.5% oxygen
atoms. A possible oxygen layer on the Al target is
thinner than 0.15 mu-g/cm2.

Data are given as the Number of Counts.
The Mg-24 targets are made from 99.98% enriched MgO,
which is reduced with Zr before evaporation onto a 0.3
mm thick Cu backing. The Mg target  contains 10 + - 2%
oxygen atoms.
